channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Why mailing a paper check has become so risky
item.description: According to a joint warning from the FBI and the U.S. Postal Service, check fraud is skyrocketing. Criminals use everyday household chemicals to “wash” away the payee’s name and dollar amount, allowing them to rewrite the check to themselves.

item.title: Trump keeps embarrassing Senate Republicans
item.description: Trump keeps embarrassing Senate Republicans

item.title: Trump's deal with Iran would end conflict without regime change. Read the full draft.
item.description: A senior U.S. official read the text for the president's memorandum of understanding to The Washington Post on the condition of anonymity.

item.title: Why fermented foods are so good for you, and 5 ways to eat more of them
item.description: Scientists have found in studies that eating fermented foods may lower inflammation, improve blood sugar control and increase the diversity of your gut microbiome, which is linked to a lower risk of developing chronic diseases.

item.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady amid red-hot inflation
item.description: It was the fourth-straight meeting in which the central bank left interest rates unchanged and the first with new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. Before his confirmation, Warsh argued the Fed had room to cut rates, a message that aligned with President Donald Trump’s long-standing preference for cheaper borrowing.

item.title: Senate postpones intelligence chief hearing after Trump derails confirmation process
item.description: GOP Sen. Tom Cotton had pledged to continue as scheduled with the hearing Wednesday, but later said that the president had “directed Jay Clayton not to appear.” Clayton was nominated to serve as director of national intelligence, replacing acting director and MAGA loyalist Bill Pulte.

item.title: Republicans thought the Senate map was their friend. It isn’t anymore.
item.description: Polls now show Democrats competitive in 10 key races — and Trump’s numbers explain why.

item.title: What’s the best way to find hidden gems in the D.C. suburbs? Ask our food critic your questions.
item.description: Washington Post food critic Elazar Sontag entertains your restaurant questions, rants and raves.

item.title: Inside a behind-the-scenes push to keep RFK Jr.’s vaccine agenda alive
item.description: The public effort to revamp U.S. vaccine policy has quieted. Out of the public eye, it’s a different story.

item.title: Death of woman freed by immigration authorities on a winter day is now ruled a homicide
item.description: Security footage shows Daphy Michel, a 31-year-old asylum seeker from Haiti, sitting on a bus stop bench for days in cold temperatures. It was at least the second death this year involving an individual who was released by federal immigration authorities and died days later of complications related to hypothermia.

item.title: A governor in today’s On the Record
item.description: A governor in today’s On the Record

item.title: Trump says intelligence chief pick Jay Clayton’s hearing is off and Bill Pulte will remain for now
item.description: President Donald Trump linked the move to his efforts to pass voting restrictions and said Clayton’s hearing would be delayed until his replacement as U.S. attorney is confirmed. Pulte will continue to serve as acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: Key takeaways from primaries in Georgia, Alabama and Oklahoma
item.description: Trump-endorsed candidates won Senate runoffs in Georgia and Alabama, but in a rare defeat, the president’s pick for Georgia governor failed to advance.

item.title: Primary results. UFC plot. White House. ICE. Developing storm. Fermented foods. Messi hat trick. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7 morning briefing

item.title: Janeese Lewis George takes sizable early lead over Kenyan McDuffie in high-stakes race to replace Bowser as D.C. mayor
item.description: The latest update from the city’s elections board put Lewis George, a democratic socialist, ahead of fellow front-runner McDuffie by double digits. The Associated Press has not yet made a call in the race.

item.title: D.C. Council member Robert C. White Jr. wins Democratic primary to succeed retiring House Del. Eleanor Holmes Norton
item.description: The District’s nonvoting representative in Congress is vacating her seat after more than three decades. A victory in the Democratic primary almost ensures White’s ascension to the seat in a city where Democrats far outnumber Republicans.

item.title: Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones, endorsed by Trump, loses Republican runoff for governor to billionaire health executive Rick Jackson
item.description: Jackson defeated Jones in a contest where the president’s influence was tested. Jackson will face Democrat Keisha Lance Bottoms, the former Atlanta mayor, in the race to succeed term-limited Gov. Brian Kemp (R).

item.title: Trump's pick wins Republican runoff in Georgia race that could determine Senate control
item.description: Trump’s endorsements, with few exceptions, have proved powerful in primary races across the country. Rep. Mike Collins defeated former football coach Derek Dooley and will face incumbent Sen. Jon Ossoff (D) in November.

item.title: Five arrested and charged with plotting to attack UFC event at the White House, Justice Department says
item.description: Authorities alleged in court papers that the plotters communicated in some detail about plans to attack the UFC event, singling out specific officials they hoped to target and mapping out locations for launching drones and escape routes. The Justice Department said the five people were arrested in four states: California, Missouri, Nebraska and Ohio.

item.title: He’s studied procrastination for 40 years. Here’s what he’s learned.
item.description: Joseph Ferrari has some thoughts about why you can’t seem to get anything done — and how to fix that.

item.title: Good luck, JD Vance. I sense a setup.
item.description: The vice president is tasked with helping the Iran regime “learn the ways of peace.”

item.title: DOJ charges 15 Minnesotans, accused of having ties to antifa, with conspiring to impede ICE
item.description: Federal prosecutors alleged that the 15 people were “conspiring to impede or injure federal officers” during the Trump administration’s immigration enforcement operation in Minneapolis earlier this year.

item.title: The funniest commencement speeches of 2026
item.description: Speakers Jane Lynch, Conan O’Brien, Rachel Dratch, Harrison Ford and Tom Brady shared wit and wisdom with college graduates.

item.title: Education Dept. plans to move offices overseeing special ed and civil rights out of the agency
item.description: The moves, described by three people briefed on the plan, are part of the Trump administration’s effort to close the Education Department by moving all of its functions to other parts of the government.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Heartbroken by breakup, and by loved ones’ meager response
item.description: She needed her loved ones to sit with her in the breakup grief – not rage at her cruel ex.

item.title: ICE removed detainee protections after private outreach from top contractor
item.description: When U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement was revising the federal standards that govern immigration detention centers, one of its top contractors privately asked for changes that could benefit its business, according to a person briefed on the discussions.

item.title: Records reveal $600M estimate for Trump’s ballroom project, with half from taxpayers
item.description: An internal cost estimate in March by the project’s contractor ran $200 million more than the president has said publicly and counters his claims that no taxpayer money will be spent.

item.title: A 9-0 Supreme Court rebuke for corner-cutting prosecutions
item.description: The justices confirm that defendants need to be tried in the place the crime was committed.

item.title: Breaking the drought in today’s On the Record
item.description: Breaking the drought in today’s On the Record

item.title: FBI thwarted attack targeting UFC event at White House, Kash Patel says
item.description: Multiple individuals have been taken into custody in connection with the alleged planned attack on the UFC event Sunday that President Donald Trump attended, the FBI director said.

item.title: Iran deal. Trump’s ballroom. Gavin Newsom. B-52 crash. Gas calculator. Antibacterial soap. World Cup kits. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Inside the zigzag bargaining that produced an Iran framework
item.description: How Kushner, Witkoff and a Qatari mediator turned an investor pitch into a fragile peace deal.

item.title: How Anthropic lost the White House’s trust — and then its flagship product
item.description: The Trump administration weighed export controls on Anthropic weeks before forcing its AI model offline, after a dispute over sharing its technology with a firm suspected of having ties to China.

item.title: As a doctor, I tell people to do these 4 things to reduce age-related muscle loss
item.description: Resistance training, protein and recovery remain the most powerful tools for preserving strength and independence later in life.

item.title: D.C. groups sue over Trump’s planned ‘Garden of American Heroes’
item.description: Six local preservation and cultural heritage organizations said work must stop on the statue garden in West Potomac Park until Congress authorizes the project.

item.title: B-52 bomber crashes in California after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base, service says
item.description: There was no immediate information on how many people were on board the aircraft, which typically carries a crew of five, at the time of the crash late Monday morning.

item.title: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says Justice Department is investigating him and his wife
item.description: The Democrat did not detail any specific allegations against him or his wife, Jennifer Siebel Newsom, but he accused President Donald Trump of weaponizing the Justice Department against him.

item.title: Meeting at Vatican last year was ‘unsettling,’ Vance writes in new book
item.description: The vice president says he preferred direct rebukes from Pope Francis to the “trite platitudes” from Vatican officials he met in April 2025, shortly before Francis's death.

item.title: This city is getting homes built twice as fast — and others want to copy it
item.description: Should every new house require a new permit for its design? Claremore, Oklahoma, does not think so.

item.title: A World Cup favorite is all settled in at a small-town Embassy Suites
item.description: As Chattanooga, Tennessee, prepared to host star-studded Spain, no detail was too small.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Troubled by her in-laws’ estrangement, and the silence around it
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Troubled by her in-laws’ estrangement, and the silence around it

item.title: This swing state shows what happens when judges need your vote
item.description: Wisconsin and other states' judicial elections are an outdated, unhealthy political practice.

item.title: The White House UFC fights showed us the America we needed to see, columnist Monica Hesse writes
item.description: Sunday night's UFC event was not a celebration of a sport, she writes, but rather a celebration of slop.

item.title: Trump sought to break Iran’s regime. He settled for reopening Hormuz.
item.description: The emerging agreement ends a costly war but leaves Iran’s leadership intact and its nuclear future still subject to negotiation.

item.title: Going public in today’s On the Record
item.description: Going public in today’s On the Record

item.title: President says 250th celebration on July 4 will be a ‘Trump rally’
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday he would be closely involved in a marquee celebration of the country’s 250th anniversary, casting the July 4th event on the National Mall as one of his political rallies.

item.title: Iran deal. UFC brawl. Rising prices. Plane crash. ADHD links. Muscle loss. Dad jokes. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Trump’s UFC event begins at the White House amid fanfare, storm threats. Follow our coverage.
item.description: The mixed martial arts fights are being held to celebrate the country’s 250th anniversary and the president’s own 80th birthday. But the forecast for the event is looking iffy.

item.title: Researchers are uncovering ADHD’s links to these other health conditions
item.description: A growing body of evidence suggests that people with ADHD may be at risk for anxiety, disordered eating, migraines, long covid and other problems.

item.title: U.S., Iran have reached a deal to end fighting, Trump and mediator Pakistan say
item.description: Trump said Sunday on Truth Social that the deal would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the U.S. naval blockade on Iranian ports. Pakistan's prime minister said an official signing ceremony will be Friday in Switzerland.
